chapter.label: Tale 7
chapter.title: The life and death of an ELDER

paragraphs.text: WHO WOULD NOT BE BIGOUMEN OF THE LAVRA OF THE TOWERS At the same Lavra of the Towers there was an elder who practised poverty to an exceptional degree® and yet his particular spiritual gift There was an ekler dwelling at the Lavra of the Towers and when was that of almsgiving. One day a beggar came to his little tower the higoumen died, the priests and other brethren at the lavra asking for alms. The elder had nothing but a single Joaf of bread wanted to make him higoumen because of his great virtue. The elder which he brought out and gave to the beggar. ‘It is not bread I begged them <not to,> saying: ‘Let me rather go and weep for my want’ said the beggar; ‘I need clothing’. This was on a Friday: He died on the Sunday morning. 10. THE LIFE OF BARNABAS THE ANCHORTTE
